message:
fix(storage): btree bounds check, ARC cache version tracking precision, MVCC GC chain fix

B-tree cursor (cursor.rs):
- `parse_cell_at` now validates the cell index against the cell pointer
  array length before indexing, returning `DatabaseCorrupt` instead of
  panicking on out-of-bounds access from corrupt pages.

ARC cache (arc_cache.rs):
- Replace per-page version *count* (`HashMap<PageNumber, usize>`) with a
  per-page version *list* (`HashMap<PageNumber, Vec<CommitSeq>>`).
- Superseded-version detection now checks whether a strictly newer version
  exists at or below the GC horizon, instead of relying on count > 1 which
  could incorrectly evict the latest version when two versions existed but
  only the newer one was below the horizon.
- `decrement_page_version` renamed to `remove_page_version` to reflect
  the precise removal semantics.

MVCC GC (gc.rs):
- `prune_page_chain_with_registry`: explicitly set `cur_idx = None` on a
  broken chain before breaking, preventing the variable from retaining
  a stale index that could confuse downstream logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
